lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY + heavy (NOT yet runtime-proven). Closes all 3 aimlops-platform writes: POST /v1/aimlops-platform (releaseaimlopsplatformv1), PUT /v1/aimlops-platform/{release_id} (updateaimlopsplatformv1), DELETE /v1/aimlops-platform/{release_id} (deleteaimlopsplatformv1). A 'release' provisions an AI/ML-Ops platform ONTO a SKE cluster — billable, slow, and tied to a real cluster_id, so this lifecycle NEVER actually provisions one. Pattern (mirrors ske-upgrade-write-coverage): soft-capture cluster_id from /v1/aimlops-platform/images or a list, soft-capture release_id from the read-only list against a DELIBERATELY-NONEXISTENT field so the {release_id} placeholder stays LITERAL at runtime -> guaranteed 404, then CALL each write once (optional+grouped+broad expect_status) to record coverage without touching a real platform. Marked heavy:true (gated behind SCP_RUN_HEAVY) as defense in depth even though it creates nothing. No data/api_bodies.json entries exist for aimlops-platform; bodies are best-effort from field semantics (release name, cluster_id, image_id, namespace, resource sizing) and are UNPROVEN. probe-reads exercises the id-bound GETs (getaimlopsplatformv1 + the cluster check/validate GETs) when a real id is present.

steps.name: list-releases
steps._note: release_id captured against a nonexistent field so the placeholder stays literal -> the PUT/DELETE/GET-by-id hit a guaranteed 404 (records coverage, touches no real platform). cluster_id soft-captured if a release exists.

steps.name: release-aimlops-platform

json.description: API regression suite — coverage-only, never provisioned

steps._note: BILLABLE platform release — never actually provisioned. Body (release_name/cluster_id/image_id/namespace) best-effort from field semantics, UNPROVEN; expected 400/422 (needs a real cluster_id/image_id + full resource spec) or 403. Deliberately NOT chained to a delete of a real release.

steps.name: update-aimlops-platform
steps._note: In-place update (updateaimlopsplatformv1) on the literal {release_id} -> 404. Body UNPROVEN (description-only guess).

steps.name: delete-aimlops-platform
steps._note: destructive — only runs under SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE. Targets the literal {release_id} (no real release) -> expected 404. optional+broad so it records coverage without deleting a real platform.

steps.name: probe-cluster-preconditions
steps._note: Stage A coverage (2026-06-14): the 5 cluster-bound precondition GETs (validate-namespaces/validate-resources/check-version + internal nodes/storageclasses) and GET-by-id need a cluster_id/release_id this coverage-only run never has (empty account -> capture_soft misses -> probe_reads filters the literal {tokens}, so they were NEVER called). Supply concrete NONEXISTENT ids so probe_reads exercises each once (-> 404/400 = endpoint reached, recorded under the catalog key) without provisioning anything. Placed BEFORE the real probe-reads so genuine captured ids, if ever present, overwrite these.
